title: perideridia kelloggii
intro: Perideridia kelloggii, also known as Kellogg's yampah or small desert parsley, is a perennial plant native to the Great Basin region of North America. It has been traditionally used in medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, with roots and leaves used to treat various ailments. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating pain, and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, the roots of Perideridia kelloggii can be used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures to utilize its medicinal properties.
